title: Fix dodge mechanic to use Pokemon repositioning instead of button press

requirements.text: Replace the dodge button/input control in the battle screen with a drag-to-reposition mechanic. The player must physically drag or move their Pokemon sprite to a new position on the battle field to dodge incoming opponent attacks. There should be no dedicated 'Dodge' button — dodging is achieved purely by repositioning the Pokemon away from the path of the incoming move.

quotes: you move your pokemon to dodge not push a button

acceptanceCriteria: No 'Dodge' button or dodge-keybind is present in the battle UI.
The player's Pokemon sprite is draggable (touch drag on mobile, click-drag on desktop) to any position within the player's side of the battle field.
When the player drags their Pokemon away from the path of an incoming opponent attack projectile, the attack misses and deals no damage.
When the player does NOT reposition their Pokemon out of the path of an incoming attack, the attack connects and deals damage normally.
Repositioning feels responsive and immediate — the Pokemon sprite follows the drag in real time with no noticeable lag.
The dodge detection uses positional overlap between the incoming attack projectile and the Pokemon sprite's current position to determine hit or miss.
The mechanic works consistently on both mobile (touch) and desktop (mouse) inputs.

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
The real-time, non-turn-based battle architecture must be preserved — do not revert to turn-based mechanics.
nonGoals: Adding any new moves, Pokemon, or story content.
Changing any other battle controls beyond the dodge mechanic.
Backend changes — this is a frontend-only interaction change.
